tasks.subject: relay-panel-ux-fixes [P0] Investigate and fix Activity tab data consistency with health bar
tasks.description:
## Objective Resolve the contradiction between the Activity tab showing no conversations while the health bar reports failures/metrics. The `useRelayConversations` hook and `useDeliveryMetrics` hook draw from different data sources. The conversations endpoint (`GET /api/relay/conversations`) builds from `relayCore.listMessages()` which stores messages in memory. The metrics endpoint aggregates from persisted counters. When the server restarts or messages are in-memory-only, the Activity tab can be empty while metrics show historical data. ## Files to Modify - `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/ActivityFeed.tsx` ## Investigation Steps Before making changes, verify the data lifecycle: 1. Check `packages/relay/src/relay-core.ts` — `listMessages()` calls `executeListMessages()` which reads from the in-memory message store (a `Map` or array). Messages are NOT persisted to SQLite. 2. Check `apps/server/src/routes/relay.ts` — the `/conversations` endpoint calls `relayCore.listMessages({})` which returns only in-memory messages from the current server process. 3. The `/metrics` endpoint (`GET /api/relay/metrics`) reads from `relayCore.getMetrics()` which may aggregate from trace data that IS persisted. This means conversations are ephemeral (lost on server restart) while metrics may persist. The empty state must be honest about this. ## Implementation: Update Empty State Copy In `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/ActivityFeed.tsx`, update the empty state (currently lines ~192-233) for the case when there are no conversations and no active filters. Replace the current empty state content: ```typescript {filteredConversations.length === 0 && !hasActiveFilters ? (
{/* Ghost preview rows -- faded placeholders showing what conversations will look like */}

No activity yet

Messages will appear here as your agents communicate

) ``` Key changes from the current code: 1. Title changed from "Waiting for messages" to "No activity yet" (clearer, not implying something is broken) 2. Description simplified to "Messages will appear here as your agents communicate" (removes the misleading implication that adapters need setup) 3. The "Set up an adapter" CTA button is REMOVED from this state entirely — it's misleading when adapters are already configured (Mode B only shows when adapters exist) 4. Ghost preview rows are kept (good UX pattern) but the icon changes from `size-10` to `size-5` for a more refined look Note: The `onSwitchToAdapters` prop is no longer used in this empty state. It can remain on the props interface for potential future use, or be removed if no other code path uses it. Check if any other rendering path uses `onSwitchToAdapters` — if not, remove it from the interface and the RelayPanel call site. ## Acceptance Criteria - When Activity tab is empty and adapters are configured, the empty state says "No activity yet" (not "Waiting for messages") - The "Set up an adapter" CTA button does not appear when adapters are already configured - Ghost preview rows are preserved for visual hint - The empty state does not contradict the health bar metrics ## Tests - Test that the empty state renders "No activity yet" text - Test that no "Set up an adapter" button appears in the empty state when in Mode B
tasks.activeForm: Investigating and fixing Activity tab data consistency with health bar

tasks.subject: relay-panel-ux-fixes [P1] Rename Failures button to Dead Letters and dialog title to Connections
tasks.description:
## Objective

Two quick labeling fixes: (1) Rename the "Failures" toggle button in ActivityFeed to "Dead Letters" to distinguish it from the "Failed" status filter dropdown, and (2) Change the dialog title from "Relay" to "Connections" in DialogHost.

## Files to Modify

- `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/ActivityFeed.tsx`
- `apps/client/src/layers/widgets/app-layout/ui/DialogHost.tsx`

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Rename "Failures" Button in ActivityFeed

In `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/ActivityFeed.tsx`, find the button (around line 152-165):

Change the button text from "Failures" to "Dead Letters" and update the aria-label:

```typescript
<Button
  variant={showFailures ? 'secondary' : 'ghost'}
  size="sm"
  className="gap-1.5"
  onClick={() => setShowFailures(!showFailures)}
  aria-pressed={showFailures}
  aria-label="Show dead letters"
>
  <AlertTriangle className="size-3.5" />
  Dead Letters
  {hasDeadLetters && !showFailures && (
    <span className="size-1.5 rounded-full bg-red-500" />
  )}
</Button>
```

Key changes:
- Text: "Failures" -> "Dead Letters"
- `aria-label`: "Show failures" -> "Show dead letters"
- Remove the `relative` class and the absolute positioning on the badge dot. Instead use `gap-1.5` on the button and make the dot inline. The current code has `className="absolute -right-0.5 -top-0.5 size-2"` on the dot - change to `className="size-1.5 rounded-full bg-red-500"` (inline, no absolute positioning needed since we're using flex gap).
- Also remove the `mr-1` class from the AlertTriangle icon since `gap-1.5` on the parent handles spacing.

### 2. Change Dialog Title in DialogHost

In `apps/client/src/layers/widgets/app-layout/ui/DialogHost.tsx`, find the relay dialog section (around line 75-88):

Change:
```typescript
<ResponsiveDialogTitle className="text-sm font-medium">Relay</ResponsiveDialogTitle>
<ResponsiveDialogDescription className="sr-only">
  Inter-agent messaging activity and endpoints
</ResponsiveDialogDescription>
```

To:
```typescript
<ResponsiveDialogTitle className="text-sm font-medium">Connections</ResponsiveDialogTitle>
<ResponsiveDialogDescription className="sr-only">
  Manage adapters and monitor message activity
</ResponsiveDialogDescription>
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- The toggle button in ActivityFeed reads "Dead Letters" (not "Failures")
- The button aria-label is "Show dead letters"
- The relay dialog title reads "Connections" (not "Relay")
- The sr-only description is updated to "Manage adapters and monitor message activity"
- Both the "Dead Letters" button and the "Failed" status filter dropdown coexist without name confusion

## Tests

- Verify "Dead Letters" text renders in ActivityFeed button
- Verify dialog title is "Connections" in DialogHost
tasks.activeForm: Renaming Failures button to Dead Letters and dialog title to Connections

tasks.subject: relay-panel-ux-fixes [P2] Add dismiss confirmation dialog to DeadLetterSection
tasks.description: ## Objective The "Dismiss All" button in `DeadLetterSection` permanently removes dead letters with a single click and no confirmation. Wrap it in an `AlertDialog` for safety, and rename from "Dismiss All" to "Mark Resolved" (less destructive language). ## Files to Modify - `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/DeadLetterSection.tsx` ## Implementation Steps ### 1. Add AlertDialog Imports Add to the imports in `DeadLetterSection.tsx`: ```typescript import { AlertDialog, AlertDialogAction, AlertDialogCancel, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogTrigger, } from '@/layers/shared/ui'; ``` Note: `Badge`, `Button`, `Dialog`, `DialogContent`, `DialogHeader`, `DialogTitle`, `DialogDescription` are already imported from `@/layers/shared/ui`. The AlertDialog components need to be added to the existing import. ### 2. Wrap the Dismiss Button In the `AggregatedCard` component, replace the current dismiss button (around lines 96-105): Current code: ```typescript ``` Replace with: ```typescript Mark dead letters as resolved? This will dismiss {group.count} dead letter{group.count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} from{' '} {group.source} ({reasonConfig.label}). This action cannot be undone. Cancel dismissMutation.mutate({ source: group.source, reason: group.reason, }) } > Mark Resolved ``` Key changes: - Button text: "Dismiss All" -> "Mark Resolved" - Button color: `text-destructive` -> `text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground` (less alarming) - Confirmation dialog with count, source, and reason details - The mutation only fires after user confirms in the dialog ## Acceptance Criteria - "Mark Resolved" button opens a confirmation dialog - Dialog shows the count, source, and reason of dead letters being dismissed - Cancel button closes dialog without dismissing - Confirm button calls `useDismissDeadLetterGroup` mutation - Button is disabled while mutation is pending ## Tests Create `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/__tests__/DeadLetterSection.test.tsx`: - Test that clicking "Mark Resolved" opens confirmation dialog (AlertDialog appears) - Test that Cancel does not trigger mutation - Test that confirming triggers the `useDismissDeadLetterGroup` mutation with correct source/reason - Test that the dialog shows correct count and source text
tasks.activeForm: Adding dismiss confirmation dialog to DeadLetterSection

tasks.subject: relay-panel-ux-fixes [P2] Show existing bindings in ConversationRow route popover
tasks.description:
## Objective The route popover in ConversationRow lets users create bindings from a conversation, but it doesn't show existing bindings for the same adapter. This risks creating duplicates. Add an info note showing existing binding count before the agent selector. ## Files to Modify - `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/ConversationRow.tsx` ## Implementation Steps ### 1. Import useBindings Hook Add the import: ```typescript import { useCreateBinding, useBindings } from '@/layers/entities/binding'; ``` (Replace the existing `useCreateBinding` import to include `useBindings`.) ### 2. Query Existing Bindings Inside the `ConversationRow` component, add: ```typescript const { data: allBindings = [] } = useBindings(); const extractedAdapterId = extractAdapterId(conversation); const existingBindings = useMemo( () => allBindings.filter((b) => b.adapterId === extractedAdapterId), [allBindings, extractedAdapterId], ); ``` ### 3. Render Existing Bindings Info in Popover In the PopoverContent (around line 228-261), add a note before the agent selector when bindings already exist for this adapter: ```typescript

Route to Agent

{existingBindings.length > 0 && (

{existingBindings.length} binding{existingBindings.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} already exist{existingBindings.length === 1 ? 's' : ''} for this adapter

)} {/* ... existing buttons ... */} ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - When opening the route popover, if the adapter already has bindings, a blue info note appears showing the count - The info note uses proper singular/plural grammar ("1 binding already exists" vs "3 bindings already exist") - The note appears above the agent selector - When the adapter has no bindings, no note appears - The `useBindings` query is deduplicated with AdapterCard's usage (same TanStack Query key) ## Tests Extend `apps/client/src/layers/features/relay/ui/__tests__/ConversationRow.test.tsx`: - Test that info note renders when `useBindings` returns bindings matching the adapter ID - Test that info note does not render when no bindings match - Test correct pluralization (1 binding vs multiple bindings)
tasks.activeForm: Showing existing bindings in ConversationRow route popover
